content.full_text:
M-88-337

AUG 24 9:01 AM 88

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

Court of Appeal
FIRST APPELLATE DISTRICT

STATE BUILDING, SAN FRANCISCO


August 22, 1988


Mr. Bruce Robeck, Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Form 721, Statement of Economic Interests

Dear Mr. Robeck:

This will confirm my secretary's conversation with you on
August 22, concerning the filing of a 721 form. It is my
understanding that it will not be necessary to file the same
since there is no break in service until March of 1989.

By copy of this letter I am also advising Ms. Frances Manfre in
Alameda County with regard to the filing of said form.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

[Signature] Confirmed 8-31-88
James F. Perley Bruce W. Robeck
Associate Justice

JP/smm

cc: Frances T. Manfre
    Alameda County Superior Court

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) must be filed given that there is no break in service until March 1989.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence TO the FPPC, not an FPPC response. It is a confirmation letter from a judicial officer referencing prior advice given by FPPC consultant. No formal Q/C sections present. High confidence in document type identification but lower confidence in question_synthetic due to lack of formal question statement.
